title: Methods and systems for automated data processing
abstract: Disclosed herein is a computer-implemented method for automatically processing data between a central repository, general ledger, distributed repository, distributed ledger, "proof of work" reward systems and/or hash algorithms with a computer system.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method for automatically processing data between a central repository, general ledger, distributed repository, distributed ledger,√¢¬Ä¬úproof of work√¢¬Ä¬ù reward systems and/or hash algorithms with a computer system, comprising: (a) detecting a plurality of characteristics of said computer system, over a computer network, to identify a plurality of actual conditions correlating to said plurality of characteristics, wherein said plurality of characteristics comprise at least a graphics processing unit (√¢¬Ä¬úGPU√¢¬Ä¬ù) utilization of said computer system and/or a central processing unit (√¢¬Ä¬úCPU√¢¬Ä¬ù) utilization of said computer system; (b) comparing, through one or more computer processors, said plurality of actual conditions with a plurality of reference conditions; (c) determining, through said one or more computer processors, whether said computer system is idle based on said plurality of actual conditions and said plurality of reference conditions; and (d) mining one or more cryptocurrencies with said computer system upon determining said computer system is idle in (c). 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said GPU utilization is less than about 10%. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said CPU utilization is less than about 20%. 4. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise a mouse movement of said computer system. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said mouse movement is less than about 5 pixels for at least about 3 minutes. 6 The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise a keyboard input of said computer system. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 6, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said keyboard input is not detected for at least about 3 minutes. 8. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise a network utilization of said computer system. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said network utilization is less than about 5%. 10. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise an inactive period of said computer system. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said inactive period is between 18:00 (Pacific Standard Time) and 6:00 (Pacific Standard Time). 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said inactive period is between 20:00 (Pacific Standard Time) and 5:00 (Pacific Standard Time). 13. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise a runtime of said computer system. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 13, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a previous runtime of said computer system. 15. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise an up-time of said computer system. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 15, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said up-time is at least about 14 hours per day. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein said plurality of reference conditions comprise a condition that said up-time is at least about 16 hours per day. 18. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise a mouse movement, a keyboard input, and a network utilization of said computer system. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein said plurality of characteristics further comprise a mouse movement, a keyboard input, a network utilization, an inactive period, a runtime, and an up-time of said computer system. 20. The computer-implemented method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein said hash algorithms comprise SHA-256d, SHA-256, CryptoNote, NeoScrypt, 1CC/2CC/TWN, ECDSA, RIPEMD160, CryptoNight, SHA3-512, Stellar Consensus Protocol (SCP), Lyra2RE, Ethash, SHA-3, Equihash, onixCoin, pharmaceutical simulations, financial batch processing, or lab sample analysis.
